probes.name: duplicate_tax_reminder_skip
probes.prompt: Evaluate whether to SEND or SKIP the following proposed reminder to the user. Here is the proposed message: 'Hey, just a reminder that you still need to pay your taxes — have you taken care of that yet?' Here is relevant chat history: [1] User said: 'Remind me to pay for my taxes'. [2] Assistant responded: 'Done! I have added a reminder for you to pay your taxes.' [3] User said: 'Ok, can you add a new priority, to keep up with my job at TechTree...' There is no acknowledgment from the user that taxes were paid. The reminder about taxes was already sent once and is still active as a task. Return SEND or SKIP with a reason.
probes.criteria:
The agent (context_analyzer) must detect that a tax reminder was already delivered and is still an active task with no user confirmation of completion. A GOOD response returns SKIP with a reason like 'duplicate reminder — tax payment reminder was already sent and is still pending, no need to re-notify'. It should NOT send the same reminder again and create spam.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #8: 'Remind me to pay for my taxes' and message #22 about removing duplicates.

probes.name: stale_diploma_task_skip
probes.prompt: Evaluate whether to SEND or SKIP the following proposed reminder to the user. Here is the proposed message: 'Don't forget to pick up your diploma from the university!' Here is relevant chat history: [1] There was an existing task: 'Pick up diploma from university'. [2] User said: 'Wait I've already picked up my diploma? Why there is task for it?' [3] Assistant confirmed the task exists. The user's statement 'I've already picked up my diploma' is a semantic acknowledgment that the task is completed. Return SEND or SKIP with a reason.
probes.criteria:
The agent (context_analyzer) must semantically match the user's statement 'I've already picked up my diploma' as a completion acknowledgment for the diploma pickup task. A GOOD response returns SKIP with a reason like 'user already confirmed diploma was picked up — reminder is stale'. The agent should not re-notify about an already-completed action.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: genuinely_new_mechanic_send
probes.prompt: Evaluate whether to SEND or SKIP the following proposed reminder to the user. Here is the proposed message: 'Reminder: you have a car mechanic appointment tomorrow at 8:00 — make sure to wake up on time!' Here is relevant chat history: [1] User said: 'I have a car mechanic visit at Friday 8:00 i need to wake up for it remind me'. [2] No prior reminder about the mechanic visit has been sent to the user. There are no duplicate or semantically similar reminders in the history. Return SEND or SKIP with a reason.

The agent (context_analyzer) must confirm that no previous reminder about the car mechanic visit was sent, and that the proposed message is the first notification fulfilling the user's explicit request. probes.name: semantic_duplicate_task_proposal
probes.prompt: Evaluate whether to SEND or SKIP the following proposed message to the user. Here is the proposed message: 'I noticed you might want to add a task about reviewing your master thesis defense questions — shall I create that for you?' Here is relevant chat history: [1] User said: 'Also please add task to review the question for my master thesis defense, as we got some example questions form university'. [2] Assistant responded: 'Created task: Review master thesis defense example questions from university.' The task already exists in the system and was confirmed created. The proposed message is semantically identical in intent. Return SEND or SKIP with a reason.
probes.criteria:
The agent (context_analyzer) must detect that the proposed message is semantically equivalent to a task the user already requested and that was already created. A GOOD response returns SKIP with a reason like 'duplicate proposal — the master thesis defense questions review task was already created per the user's earlier request; re-asking would be redundant and spammy'. The agent must do semantic matching, not just keyword matching, since the wording differs but the intent is the same.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #6: 'Also please add task to review the question for my master thesis defense, as we got some example questions form university' and message #14 about duplicate tasks.
